verses.text: I fed you with milk, not meat, for ye are 'yet carnal,' with envying and strife. Babes still. Their quarrels prove their immaturity.
I fed you with milk, not meat, for ye are 'yet carnal,' with envying and strife. Babes still. Their quarrels prove their immaturity.
I fed you with milk, not meat, for ye are 'yet carnal,' with envying and strife. Babes still. Their quarrels prove their immaturity.
Who is Paul, who Apollos? ministers; 'I have planted, Apollos watered; but God gave the increase.' God's work. The fruit is God's, not the labourers'.
Who is Paul, who Apollos? ministers; 'I have planted, Apollos watered; but God gave the increase.' God's work. The fruit is God's, not the labourers'.
Who is Paul, who Apollos? ministers; 'I have planted, Apollos watered; but God gave the increase.' God's work. The fruit is God's, not the labourers'.
Who is Paul, who Apollos? ministers; 'I have planted, Apollos watered; but God gave the increase.' God's work. The fruit is God's, not the labourers'.
We are 'labourers together with God'; ye are 'God's husbandry... God's building.' His field. The church is God's own work and dwelling.
We are 'labourers together with God'; ye are 'God's husbandry... God's building.' His field. The church is God's own work and dwelling.
As a wise builder I laid the foundation; 'other foundation can no man lay than... Jesus Christ.' One foundation. All must build on Christ alone.
As a wise builder I laid the foundation; 'other foundation can no man lay than... Jesus Christ.' One foundation. All must build on Christ alone.
Every man's work shall be 'tried... by fire'; the day shall declare it. Tested. The quality of our work will be revealed at last.
Every man's work shall be 'tried... by fire'; the day shall declare it. Tested. The quality of our work will be revealed at last.
If his work abide, he is rewarded; if burned, he suffers loss, 'yet so as by fire.' Loss but saved. Poor work is lost, though the worker is spared.
If his work abide, he is rewarded; if burned, he suffers loss, 'yet so as by fire.' Loss but saved. Poor work is lost, though the worker is spared.
'Ye are the temple of God'; the Spirit dwells in you; God will destroy him that defiles it. Holy temple. The church is sacred to God.
'Ye are the temple of God'; the Spirit dwells in you; God will destroy him that defiles it. Holy temple. The church is sacred to God.
Let no man deceive himself; 'the wisdom of this world is foolishness with God.' Vain wisdom. Worldly cleverness counts for nothing with Him.
Let no man deceive himself; 'the wisdom of this world is foolishness with God.' Vain wisdom. Worldly cleverness counts for nothing with Him.
Let no man deceive himself; 'the wisdom of this world is foolishness with God.' Vain wisdom. Worldly cleverness counts for nothing with Him.
Let no man glory in men; 'all things are yours; and ye are Christ's; and Christ is God's.' Rich heirs. In Christ the believer possesses all.
